content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-4441

June 5, 1985

Richard E. Marcus
Green & Goren
3340 Ocean Park Blvd.
Santa Monica, CA 90405

Re: FPPC Advice No. A-84-210

Dear Mr. Marcus:

Thank you for your letter dated May 8, 1985, in which you
inquired about the reply to your request for advice dated
August 15, 1984. I am at a loss to explain why you did not receive
a reply to your August 15, 1984 letter, and I hope the oversight
did not cause you any inconvenience.

You asked about the proper method of reporting a payment of
$4,727.80 by the Friends of Alatorre, and the subsequent
reimbursement of the same amount received by the Friends of
Alatorre. You indicated that the Friends of Alatorre paid
$4,7276.80 to Cerrell Associates, Inc. for the costs of a slate
mailer, with the understanding that Friends of Alatorre would be
reimbursed. The entire amount received by Cerrell Associates was
paid to Below, Tobe & Associates for printing costs. Friends of
Alatorre was reimbursed the entire amount as follows:

(1) $3,972.80 by Cerrell Associates, which represented
payments received by four other candidates for their
share of the costs of the slate mailer;

(2) $250 was paid directly to Friends of Alatorre by two
other committees for their share of the costs of the
mailer.

Friends of Alatorre should report the payment and
reimbursement of the $4,727.80 in the following manner:

On Schedule E of the Form 490: Itemize the expenditure of
$4,727.80 to Cerrell Associates, Inc. In addition, pursuant to
Gov. Code Section 84303, since Cerrell Associates was acting as an

Richard E. Marcus
June 5, 1985
Page 2


agent or independent contractor on behalf of the Friends of
Alatorre, the committee must also itemize the payment to Below,
Toche. (Please refer to pages 36-37 of the "Information Manual on
Campaign Disclosure Provisions" for an explanation of how to report
payments made by agents and independent contractors.)

On Schedule G of the Form 490 (Miscellaneous Adjustments to
Cash Position"): Itemize receipt of the reimbursement of the
$4,727.80, and for each payment received, itemize the source of the
payment, the amount, and provide a description (e.g., "reimbursement for cost of slate mailer").

Please contact me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

[illegible]
Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

GREEN, GOREN & HOWARD
AN ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION

SANDY A. GREEN, CPA
JAN L. GOREN, CPA
DANIEL M. HOWARD, CPA
MERLYN K JOHNSON, CPA
RICHARD E. MARCUS, CPA

FPPC

Aug 17 11:28 AM '84 4200 OCEAN PARK BOULEVARD, SUITE 3070
SANTA MONICA, CALIFORNIA 90405
TELEPHONE (213) 450-8355

August 15, 1984


Jean Pritchard
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Jean:

We are requesting the advice of the legal division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission regarding proper presentation on the Consolidated
Campaign Statement, Form 490, for the following fact situation:

Friends of Aletorre (hereinafter referred to as FOA), a
"controlled committee", paid $4,727.80 to Correll Associates, Inc.
(hereinafter referred to as Correll) for the cost of a slate
mailer. Correll had paid the exact amount to Below, Tobe &
Associates for printing costs. Correll reimbursed FOA $3,927.80,
which represented funds collected from four candidates for their
share of the slate mailer costs. The remaining $800 was reimbursed
to FOA as follows:

1. $250 was paid by Correll on behalf of another
   candidate.

2. $550 was paid directly from two other committees for
   their share of the slate mailer expense.

The slate mailer was compiled and mailed in the conventional
manner. The Correll group approached FOA and informed them that
they would put together a mailer and pay for it if it was approved
by FOA. FOA and the Correll group decided upon whom to include on
the slate. Once Correll had compiled the slate mailer, it was
telecopied to the FOA group for their approval. FOA suggested
slight changes to the mailer before final approval. The Correll
group made all arrangements for the mailer except for printing.

GREEN, GOREN & HOWARD
AN ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION


Jean Pritchard
August 15, 1984
Page 2




Please inform us as to the manner in which these facts should be presented on
the Consolidated Campaign Statement.

If you have questions, please do not hesitate to call.  Thank you.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

RICHARD E. MARCUS
Green, Goren & Howard

jk

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901         322-6441

August 17, 1984


Richard E. Marcus
Green, Coren & Howard
3340 Ocean Park Boulevard, Suite 3070
Santa Monica, CA 90405

Re: A-84-210

Dear Mr. Marcus:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
has been referred to Jeanne Pritchard, a Political Reform
Consultant in the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division of
the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any
questions about your advice request, you may contact
Ms. Pritchard directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, you
should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM:plh

sections.question_synthetic: How should Friends of Alatorre report on Form 490 a $4,727.80 payment to Cerrell Associates for a slate mailer and the subsequent reimbursement of that amount from multiple candidates and committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Friends of Alatorre must itemize the $4,727.80 expenditure to Cerrell Associates on Schedule E, and also itemize the underlying payment to Below, Tobe per Government Code Section 84303 since Cerrell acted as an agent. The reimbursements must be itemized on Schedule G with source, amount, and description for each payment received.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Complete advice letter with incoming request letter and initial referral letter included. OCR quality appears good. Document includes both the original inquiry dated August 15, 1984 and the response dated June 5, 1985 explaining an administrative oversight in not responding timely. Minor OCR errors noted (e.g., '$4,7276.80' appears to be '$4,727.80', '$3,927.80' vs '$3,972.80' in different sections of document).

CONCLUSION: Friends of Alatorre should report the payment and reimbursement of the $4,727.80 in the following manner: On Schedule E of the Form 490: Itemize the expenditure of $4,727.80 to Cerrell Associates, Inc. In addition, pursuant to Gov. Code Section 84303, since Cerrell Associates was acting as an agent or independent contractor on behalf of the Friends of Alatorre, the committee must also itemize the payment to Below, Tobe. On Schedule G of the Form 490 (Miscellaneous Adjustments to Cash Position): Itemize receipt of the reimbursement of the $4,727.80, and for each payment received, itemize the source of the payment, the amount, and provide a description (e.g., "reimbursement for cost of slate mailer").

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-84-210 addresses the proper reporting on Form 490 for a slate mailer expense initially paid by Friends of Alatorre to Cerrell Associates and subsequently reimbursed by multiple candidates and committees. The FPPC provides detailed instructions on scheduling the expenditure and reimbursements with required agent disclosure.
